Create a Single OpenAI Batch API - Chat Completions Request

Source: R/openai_batch_api.R
oai_batch_build_completions_req.Rd

This function prepares a single row of data for the OpenAI Batch/Files APIs, where each row should be valid JSON. The APIs do not guarantee the results will be in the same order, so we need to provide an ID with each request.

Usage

oai_batch_build_completions_req(
  input,
  id,
  model = "gpt-4o-mini",
  system_prompt = NULL,
  temperature = 0,
  max_tokens = 500L,
  schema = NULL,
  method = "POST",
  endpoint = "/v1/chat/completions"
)

Arguments

input

Text input (user message) for the completion

id

A custom, unique row ID

model

The chat completion model to use

system_prompt

Optional system prompt to guide the model's behaviour

temperature

Sampling temperature (0 = deterministic, higher = more random)

max_tokens

Maximum number of tokens to generate

schema

Optional JSON schema for structured output (json_schema object or list)

method

The HTTP request type, usually 'POST'

endpoint

The API endpoint path, e.g. /v1/chat/completions

Value

A row of JSON suitable for the Batch API

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
req <- oai_batch_build_completions_req(
  input = "What is the capital of France?",
  id = "query_1",
  model = "gpt-4o-mini",
  temperature = 0
)
} # }
